newsletters.title: We Built Our Own Agent-native Tool. It Overhauled How We Build Software.
newsletters.content:
A solution for our chaotic Monday meetings changed what we ship customers  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2UgQnVpbHQgT3VyIE93biBBZ2VudC1uYXRpdmUgVG9vbC4gSXQgT3ZlcmhhdWxlZCBIb3cgV2UgQnVpbGQgU29mdHdhcmUuIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMywicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9


WE BUILT OUR OWN AGENT-NATIVE TOOL. IT OVERHAULED HOW WE BUILD SOFTWARE. https://every.


A SOLUTION FOR OUR CHAOTIC MONDAY MEETINGS CHANGED WHAT WE SHIP CUSTOMERS https://every.

by Stella Garber https://every.

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Stella Garber https://every. cofounded Hoop, an AI agent to help subcriber brands cut churn, after years at Trello watching what makes software stick. When her team’s customer discovery calls became a mess of scattered notes and competing interpretations, they built an internal AI analysis tool from scratch using Every’s agent-native architecture https://every. philosophy. It reshaped how they build their actual product. Plus: While you’re waiting for Fable 5 to return, we’ve compiled 13 copy-ready prompts https://every. based on the Every team’s workflows. Use them to plan, build, research, verify, and hand off complex work that runs for hours.—Kate Lee https://every.

Get the Fable prompts https://every.



----------------------------------------



It was Monday morning, and my cofounder Brian was reading from our agent’s weekly analysis of customer discovery calls. “Subscription retention,” he said. “Five separate brands mentioned it as their top priority, and none of them trust existing AI tools to touch it.”

Just weeks ago, unearthing an insight like this would’ve been nearly impossible.

At my pre-product-market fit startup, we’d all been speaking with prospects and trying to figure out the positioning for our product, but keeping track of everything we learned was a mess across founders, platforms, and mediums. To share what we learned during our Monday meeting, Brian would read notes in Slack, collect transcripts from Granola, and try to make sense of it all in Claude Code.

We couldn’t afford to be that disorganized. We’d recently launched Hoop https://every., an agent that helps subscription brands reduce churn, and we needed to learn as quickly as we could. So we had to talk to as many potential customers as possible, then rigorously document and score each call to separate polite interest from genuine demand. Everyone on our five-person team was putting in the effort, but each of us had our own process, our own tools, and our own interpretation of what happened on each customer call.

So my two cofounders and I—none of us with “engineer” in our title—built an internal tool to fix it. What we didn’t expect was that the tool would change how we built our actual product for customers, too.

https://every.




THROW OUT THE OLD PLAYBOOK

The software development lifecycle wasn’t designed for agentic AI—and it shows. Tools, decisions, and the relationship with code itself are shifting faster than any playbook accounts for.

Meet Command Line https://every., Microsoft’s new blog straight from their technical teams, sharing what they’re building, how they’re operating, and what they’re learning along the way. From articles like why the industry keeps confusing AI capability with AI reality to how they’re designing agent-first devices from scratch, Command Line is written by builders, for builders.

Read now https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiV2UgQnVpbHQgT3VyIE93biBBZ2VudC1uYXRpdmUgVG9vbC4gSXQgT3ZlcmhhdWxlZCBIb3c

newsletters.title: Anthropic vs government
newsletters.content:
~[Sign up](https://www.theaivalley.com/)~~ | ~~[Follow us on X](https://twitter.com/heyBarsee)~~ | ~~[Sponsor](mailto:barsee@aivalley.ai)~

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c167fb98-39eb-42d4-a114-56fe02753cac/2__1_-min__2_-min.png?t=1744033912)
Follow image link: (https://www.theaivalley.com/subscribe)
Caption: 

Together with

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/bae2c2e2-901b-4ac1-8306-4fabd5cebb57/adobe-firefly-logo__1_.png?t=1781620040)
Follow image link: (https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/06/creators-toolkit-report-2026)
Caption: 



----------
==howdy, it’s Barsee again.==

==happy tuesday, AI family, and welcome back to AI Valley.==

==**here are the biggest things worth knowing today:**==

* Claude Fable 5 was switched off by the US government

* SpaceX acquires AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion

* Plus trending AI tools, posts, and resources

Let’s _dive_ into the Valley of AI…


----------

----------
#### **ADOBE**

##### Creative AI is doing more than changing how creators work. It’s also shaping how they think about what’s possible

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3a4935b0-f1ad-44bb-919d-57ff7240c8e7/Screenshot_2026-06-16_at_10.11.48_AM__1_.png?t=1781620024)
Follow image link: (https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/06/creators-toolkit-report-2026)
Caption: Courtesy: Adobe

According to Adobe’s latest Creators’ Toolkit Report, nearly half (48%) of creators say that creative AI makes them feel more secure about their future as creators. In addition, 40% say that AI-assisted content consistently performs better.  Learn more on [Adobe’s newsroom.](https://news.adobe.com/news/2026/06/creators-toolkit-report-2026)

~_*This is sponsored_~


----------

----------
#### **THROUGH THE VALLEY**

##### 1/ Claude Fable 5 was switched off by the US government

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/a2eecd65-1474-4ae0-baf7-e848a3184074/Anthropic-Says-US-Gov-Ordered-it-to-Shut-Down-Mythos-Models-Business-2280583841__1_.png?t=1781622310)
Caption: 

More than 100 cybersecurity executives and researchers have [signed](https://freefable.org/) an open letter urging the U.S. government to lift restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The move comes just days after Anthropic launched Fable 5, its new Mythos-class model designed for general use. On June 12, the company abruptly disabled both models after receiving a government directive restricting access for foreign nationals, including international customers, researchers, and even some Anthropic employees.

The [restrictions](https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access) followed concerns that a jailbreak could allow the models to identify software vulnerabilities and bypass some cybersecurity safeguards. The government order reportedly restricted access for foreign nationals, including international customers, researchers, and even some Anthropic employees. Unable to enforce those restrictions cleanly, Anthropic shut the models off entirely.

**Why it matters:**

Fable 5 went from one of the most anticipated AI launches of the year to being unavailable worldwide in less than three days. That's a level of government intervention the AI industry has rarely seen.

The dispute could shape how future frontier models are released. If advanced AI systems can face restrictions after launch, companies may need to spend as much time working with regulators and national security agencies as they do building the technology itself.

##### 2/ SpaceX acquires AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1c2ee5c6-b667-425a-9f4a-ceb335fbd15d/675664_624347_5551__1_.png?t=1781621236)
Caption: 

SpaceX has agreed to [acquire](https://x.com/BullTheoryio/status/2066836174249852977?s=20) AI coding startup Cursor in a $60 billion stock deal, just days after its blockbuster IPO. Cursor has become one of the fastest-growing software companies in history, with millions of developers using its AI-powered coding tools to write, edit, and manage software. Before the deal, the company was reportedly raising funding at a $50 billion valuation and had attracted backing from Andreessen Horowitz, Thrive Capital, and Nvidia.

**Why it matters:**

SpaceX has told investors that AI could become one of its biggest businesses in the future, with the company estimating a $26 trillion opportunity across AI infrastructure and software. Cursor gives SpaceX a product already used by developers around the world and a direct foothold in one of the fastest-growing parts of the AI market.


--------------------
# TRENDING TOOLS


--------------------
* [Runway](https://x.com/runwayml/status/2066596138824696214?s=20) > The AI video generator is now available directly inside OpenAI's ChatGPT

* [Uplift](https://getuplift.ai/) > Connects with your existing tools to build, deploy, and manage custom AI agents that automate repetitive workflows across your team

* [Taste Lab](https://www.tastelab.xyz/) > Analyzes any website's design DNA and transforms it into comprehensive token maps and reasoning briefs for AI-powered agents


----------

----------
# WHAT I'M CONSUMING


--------------------
* Inside Cursor's [wild rise](https://web.archive.org/web/20260616021200/https://www.businessinsider.com/cursor-ceo-michael-truell-spacex-elon-musk-anthropic-2026-6)

* The web we know is going to [disappear](https://www.minid.net/2026/6/15/the-web-is-going-to-dissapear)

* SpaceX & the [Sentient Sun](https://x.com/pmarca/status/2066523671456579828)


----------

----------
# THE VALLEY GEMS


--------------------
__What’s trending on social today:__

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/mntruell/status/2066874098001883538?s=20)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/jamesdouma/status/2066553216817578202?s=20)


----------

----------
# THAT’S ALL FOR TODAY


--------------------
Thank you for reading today’s edition. That’s all for today’s issue.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/e1f6264a-ca6c-46cf-ab92-be1134d19541/funny-my-favorite-dog-gifs-16__1_.gif?t=1715766102)
Caption: 

💡 Help me get better and suggest new ideas at [barsee@aivalley.ai](mailto:barsee@aivalley.ai) or [@heyBarsee](https://twitter.com/heyBarsee)

👍️ New reader? [Subscribe here](https://www.theaivalley.com/)

Thanks for being here.


--------------------

--------------------

Plus: Everything we published about Anthropic's new model  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

Context Window

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3ciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


FABLE, DISABLED https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZmFibGUtZGlzYWJsZWQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Mn0=


PLUS: EVERYTHING WE PUBLISHED ABOUT ANTHROPIC'S NEW MODEL https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvZmFibGUtZGlzYWJsZWQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Every Staff https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQEV2ZXJ5JTIwU3RhZmYiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

On Friday night, the U.S. government banned Anthropic’s distribution of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 to non-U.S. nationals. In response, Anthropic disabled Fable for all customers. As of this writing, the situation is ongoing.

It remains to be seen how it will play out, but I can already see the difference in my AI usage. Here’s a graph comparing my Claude and Codex usage before and after the ban (the “event” below):

https://every. courtesy of Dan Shipper.




Before the ban, I was split about evenly between Claude and Codex. After (and after a period where I was using neither because I was sleeping), I switched almost entirely to Codex.

My guess is that this ban is not going to last very long. It seems to rest on a misunderstanding between the government and Anthropic https://every. about which kinds of guardrail bypasses are fixable and what counts as an adequate solution. Anthropic believes the jailbreak identified by the government is narrow rather than universal—it surfaces only minor vulnerabilities that other public models are already susceptible to. The government apparently believes otherwise. Because both sides are highly incentivized to work this out, I’d bet that the ban is revoked after a few days—and demand for the newly returned Fable skyrockets.

However, this kind of move is extremely disruptive and distracting for people working at Anthropic. The only comparable scenario I can remember is Sam Altman https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZXZlcnl0aGluZy90aGlua3NnaXZpbmctaXMtdXBvbi11cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ==’s firing https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZXZlcnl0aGluZy90aGlua3NnaXZpbmctaXMtdXBvbi11cyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo4fQ==, which was resolved relatively quickly. Even though Altman was reinstated, I do think the chaos disrupted the company’s momentum for months afterward.

We’ll keep a close eye on whether the same is true here.—Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGRhbnNoaXBwZXIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vYWNjb3VudCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMH0= to get it in your inbox.

----------------------------------------


KNOWLEDGE BASE

“Vibe Check: Fable 5 Is the Best Coding Model in the World” https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9hbnRocm9waWMtbXl0aG9zLW91ci1mYWJsZS12aWJlLWNoZWNrIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ== by Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGRhbnNoaXBwZXIiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTJ9 and Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxM30=/Vibe Check https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjayIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNH0=: Anthropic’s Fable 5, the first model in its Mythos class, tops every model Every has tested on coding. It’s also overpowered, and overpriced, for most everyday work. Dan and Katie Parrott https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGthdGllLnBhcnJvdHQxMiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNX0= ran it through Every’s standard evaluations and landed on one rule: Reach for it on long, complex, minimally supervised tasks, and stay with GPT-5.5 or Opus 4.8 for the rest. Read this for the head-to-head verdict and when Fable 5 is worth the cost.

“How to Get the Most Out of Fable 5” https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvaG93LXRvLWdldC10aGUtbW9zdC1vdXQtb2YtZmFibGUtNSIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNn0= by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiRmFibGUsIERpc2FibGVkIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMSwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8v

newsletters.title: The Moral of Fable
newsletters.content:
What the most powerful model yet teaches us about the future of knowledge work  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjB9

Chain of Thought

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY2hhaW4tb2YtdGhvdWdodC90aGUtbW9yYWwtb2YtZmFibGU_bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MX0=


THE MORAL OF FABLE https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY2hhaW4tb2YtdGhvdWdodC90aGUtbW9yYWwtb2YtZmFibGU_bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6Mn0=


WHAT THE MOST POWERFUL MODEL YET TEACHES US ABOUT THE FUTURE OF KNOWLEDGE WORK https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY2hhaW4tb2YtdGhvdWdodC90aGUtbW9yYWwtb2YtZmFibGU_bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6M30=

by Dan Shipper https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY2hhaW4tb2YtdGhvdWdodC90aGUtbW9yYWwtb2YtZmFibGU_bWV0ZXJlZF9wYXl3YWxsPTEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vYWNjb3VudCIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo1fQ== to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------



As any child who’s heard Aesop knows, the point of a fable is its moral. We see the consequences of falsely crying wolf. We learn why slow and steady wins the hare race.

So what is the moral of Claude Fable 5, Anthropic’s newest model, which this week we called the best coding model in the world https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vdmliZS1jaGVjay9hbnRocm9waWMtbXl0aG9zLW91ci1mYWJsZS12aWJlLWNoZWNrIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9?

For engineers, the case is easy to make. For many knowledge workers, though, Fable might feel incremental. You may have one-shotted an impressive demo or two, but you’re probably not using it for your day-to-day work. Why would you? It costs twice as much and the results aren’t that much better.

But there is a certain class of developers who are feeling Fable’s full force. These are people like Cora https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vY29yYS5jb21wdXRlciIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ== general manager Kieran Klaassen https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vQGtpZXJhbl8xMzU1IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjh9, who are suddenly churning through his backlog of bug fixes and feature requests in hours instead of days. “This is my favorite model ever,” he told me.

What’s the difference between Kieran and everyone else? The difference between Kieran and most people using Fable isn’t simply that he’s a developer but that he’s at Level 7 or 8 on our scale of AI use https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vZ3VpZGVzL3RoZS1laWdodC1sZXZlbHMtb2YtYWktYWRvcHRpb24iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0=: He delegates whole projects, lets agents work asynchronously, reviews the results, and feeds what he learns into the next run. In other words he writes—dare I say it, loops https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8veC5jb20vc3RlaXBldGUvc3RhdHVzLzIwNjM2OTcxNjI3NDgyNjA2MjciLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTB9—not prompts.

For now, this might make Fable seem like a tool for developers. But in AI, developer workflows have a habit https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vY29udGV4dC13aW5kb3cvb25lLWFwcC10by1ydWxlLWFsbC1rbm93bGVkZ2Utd29yayIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxMX0= of spreading to the rest of knowledge work. Claude Code started as a developer tool, and now the same methodology is being used for everything from slide decks to spreadsheets https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vYWxzby10cnVlLWZvci1odW1hbnMveW91LWFyZS10aGUtbW9zdC1leHBlbnNpdmUtbW9kZWwiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTJ9 inside of Cowork and Codex.

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vYi5saW5rL2V2ZXJ5LW4zIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEzLCJhZHZlcnRpc2VtZW50X2lkIjoxMTgxfQ==

Cost-per-database pricing limits your creativity. You have a single instance, change things carefully, and are limited to one or two experiments. But Ghost is different. It’s Postgres designed for agents that need unlimited databases on demand. Spin up 20 copies for testing, fork for each experiment, and delete what fails. Your agent handles everything: authentication, introspection, and queries, via native MCP integration. Built specifically for Claude Code and agentic workflows. Free tier: unlimited databases, 1TB storage.

Get started https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vYi5saW5rL2V2ZXJ5LW4zP3NvdXJjZT1wb3N0X2J1dHRvbiIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxNCwiYWR2ZXJ0aXNlbWVudF9pZCI6MTE4MX0=
Want to sponsor Every? Click here https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Im1haWx0bzpzcG9uc29yc2hpcHNAZXZlcnkudG8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTV9.

If you’re not feeling Fable’s force, that’s probably because you haven’t yet started to treat your work like gardening ...



----------------------------------------



Become a paid subscriber to Every https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiVGhlIE1vcmFsIG9mIEZhYmxlIiwicG9zdF9pZCI6NDMwMCwicG9zdF90eXBlIjoicG9zdCIsInVybCI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXZlcnkudG8vc3Vic

newsletters.title: RSVP: Push Fable and Codex to the max
newsletters.content:
​

( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/dpheh0hegp0le8hmh4/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50bw== )

Hi there,

Anthropic’s Fable 5 can ​finish projects that once took days or weeks ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/e0hph7h7gr0874i8h2/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by92aWJlLWNoZWNrL2FudGhyb3BpYy1teXRob3Mtb3VyLWZhYmxlLXZpYmUtY2hlY2s= )​. It’s also slow and expensive for everyday work. So which jobs are worth it?

This Friday, the Every team is holding a live two-hour camp to answer that question with the actual projects we’ve built with the best coding model in the world.

​Fable 5 Power User Camp ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/7qh7h8h97nor93hzh6/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9ldmVudHMvZmFibGUtNS1wb3dlci11c2VyLWNhbXA= )​
​Friday, June 12
12–2 p.m. ET
Live on Zoom

-->Upgrade to join ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/p8heh9h40qzn46iqh3/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9zdWJzY3JpYmU= )
Upgrade to join ( https://every.to/subscribe )​Dan Shipper​, ​Kieran Klaassen​, ​Katie Parrott​, ​Austin Tedesco​, and ​Nityesh Agarwal​ will walk through:

* The projects Fable completed from start to finish
* How we use it for coding, growth, research, and writing
* The prompts, context, and review loops behind the results
* When we reach for Fable versus Codex

The camp, recording, and our full Vibe Check are included with a paid Every subscription. Paid subscribers also will get access to our dedicated Codex Power User Camp ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/6qheh8hlg97mlgiohk/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9ldmVudHMvY29kZXgtcG93ZXItdXNlci1jYW1w ) on Friday, June 26.

-->Save your seat ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/p8heh9h40qzn46iqh3/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9zdWJzY3JpYmU= )
Save your seat ( https://every.to/subscribe )See you Friday,
The Every team

You received this email because you signed up for emails from Every ( https://0ab9ee3d.click.kit-mail3.com/0vu5mv645et9h96eepqflhv7ooo9wcnh5ngmn/dpheh0hegp0le8hmh4/aHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50bw== ). No longer interested in receiving emails from us? Click here to

newsletters.title: AI Everywhere, All at Once
newsletters.content:
Fable 5 versus all other models, a Fable prompt starter pack, and an inside look at Apple’s developer conference  ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌
https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by8iLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MH0=

Context Window

https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9jb250ZXh0LXdpbmRvdyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjoxfQ==


AI EVERYWHERE, ALL AT ONCE https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9jb250ZXh0LXdpbmRvdy9haS1ldmVyeXdoZXJlLWFsbC1hdC1vbmNlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjJ9


FABLE 5 VERSUS ALL OTHER MODELS, A FABLE PROMPT STARTER PACK, AND AN INSIDE LOOK AT APPLE’S DEVELOPER CONFERENCE https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9jb250ZXh0LXdpbmRvdy9haS1ldmVyeXdoZXJlLWFsbC1hdC1vbmNlIiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjN9

by Laura Entis https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AbGF1cmFfMjdiYmFmXzEiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NH0=

Midjourney/Every illustration.

Anthropic’s Mythos-level Fable 5 is here https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by92aWJlLWNoZWNrL2FudGhyb3BpYy1teXRob3Mtb3VyLWZhYmxlLXZpYmUtY2hlY2siLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6NX0=, which means we’re experimenting with how to get the most of the super-capable, token-hungry model. Today, four Every team members share their approaches, plus we package eight Fable workflows into prompts you can test out for yourself https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9wL2NsYXVkZS1mYWJsZS01LXByb21wdC1saWJyYXJ5IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjZ9. Elsewhere, Monologue https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubW9ub2xvZ3VlLnRvLyIsInBvc2l0aW9uIjo3fQ== general manager Naveen Naidu https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AbmF2ZWVuXzY4MDQiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OH0= reports from the ground at Apple’s developer conference on why Siri is—wait for it—finally good, and head of platform Willie Williams https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9Ad2lsbGlld2lsbGlhbXMiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6OX0= argues the one thing even the most powerful LLMs can’t do is vibe.

Was this newsletter forwarded to you? Sign up https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9hY2NvdW50IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjEwfQ== to get it in your inbox.



----------------------------------------




INSIDE EVERY

FABLE 5 VERSUS EVERYTHING ELSE

Anytime there’s a major new model release, there’s pressure to reconsider your AI setup. Or, if you’ve just come out of a meditation retreat, maybe your entire life https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly94LmNvbS9kYW5zaGlwcGVyL3N0YXR1cy8yMDExNzkxODAyNTUwOTIzNTc5IiwicG9zaXRpb24iOjExfQ==.

Should you swap out your preferred model for the newest arrival? Is the new model sufficiently better to make the switch if you don’t like the harness https://every.?

Fable 5 has thrown the Every team into a new round of existential questioning. It’s an obvious first choice for certain projects—those that are large, complex, and

delegable—and an arguably worse, too-expensive fit for others.

After a week of testing the model, most of us at Every have settled into a two-prong approach: Fire up Fable for ambitious assignments, let it do its thing, and reach for your favored coding agent for smaller-scale, iterative tasks.

Head of growth Austin Tedesco https://every.to/emails/click/1eb47536f45a3508ca723b5b2cb780e942839f966e324ef3162cd49469aec49f/eyJzdWJqZWN0IjoiQUkgRXZlcnl3aGVyZSwgQWxsIGF0IE9uY2UiLCJwb3N0X2lkIjo0Mjk4LCJwb3N0X3R5cGUiOiJwb3N0IiwidXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9ldmVyeS50by9AdGVkZXNjYXUiLCJwb3NpdGlvbiI6MTN9’s breakdown: Fable 5 demands “a very different way of approaching knowledge work,” one that requires fine-tuning exactly what outcomes you want from the model, what information it needs to execute, and trusting it enough to sit back and let it cook.
